title: Earliest Finish Time for Land and Water Rides II
performanceTarget: top 1% execution time and overall performance
note: Pareto-optimal challenge: the single recommended solution is also the speed extreme and the memory extreme. Per the repo standard (no coincident files), only solution.cpp is shipped; there is no separate solution-runtime or solution-memory file.

variants.languageReason: Native, GC-free int arithmetic over vector<int>; pure min/max/+ workload needs no containers, so C++ ties C on speed and sits at the O(1)-extra memory floor while staying readable. Top 1% on both axes.
variants.approachJustification: Monotonicity insight: for each order, the best first ride is the one with the minimum finish time, so two independent linear scans per order replace the O(n*m) pairing. Also serves as the speed and memory extreme.

reasoningSummary: Rides are from different categories, so the first and second picks are independent. For a fixed order and fixed second ride, total finish max(firstFinish, secondStart)+secondDuration is non-decreasing in firstFinish, so only the minimum-finish first ride matters. Precompute bestLandFinish and bestWaterFinish, scan the opposite list once per order, take the min over both orders. Linear time and O(1) space dominate the hinted sort+binary-search approach, making this Pareto-optimal.
